source: Curated from JobsDB HK, Glassdoor HK, CTgoodjobs, FDM Group graduate programme listings, and LinkedIn HK job postings (2025-2026)

roles.growthOutlook: Demand growing ~15% YoY in HK, driven by fintech and smart city initiatives. Strong demand across banking, insurance, and retail sectors. Entry-level roles frequently open to fresh graduates.

requiredSkills.description: Write complex queries with joins, subqueries, and window functions for data extraction and transformation. Used daily in most data analyst roles.
Use pandas and numpy for data manipulation; matplotlib/seaborn for visualization; basic scripting for automation and reporting.
Build interactive dashboards, design KPI reports, and present data insights to business stakeholders.
Advanced formulas, pivot tables, Power Query, and VBA macros for ad-hoc analysis. Still the most common tool for business reporting in HK.
Hypothesis testing, confidence intervals, regression analysis, and experimental design for business decision-making.
Statistical computing language. Valued in clinical research and some financial analytics roles. Python often preferred in HK tech sector.
Understanding of data extraction, transformation, and loading workflows. Increasingly expected as data infrastructure matures.
Familiarity with cloud data services (Azure Data Factory, AWS Glue, BigQuery) is a strong differentiator for entry-level candidates.
Present findings to non-technical stakeholders, write clear analytical reports, and create executive summaries.
Important for stakeholder interaction in local HK companies and government projects. Less critical at MNCs but opens more job opportunities.
Version control for collaborative analytics projects. Increasingly expected in tech-forward data teams.
Useful for roles involving cross-border Greater Bay Area projects or mainland Chinese clients.

roles.growthOutlook: Steady demand in HK, particularly in fintech, virtual banking, and enterprise software. Graduate programmes available from FDM, Accenture, and major banks. Remote/hybrid work increasingly common.

requiredSkills.description: Most commonly requested language in HK enterprise and banking software roles. Spring Boot framework knowledge expected.
Used for backend services, scripting, automation, and increasingly for AI/ML integration in applications.
Core for web development. React.js and Node.js are the most in-demand frameworks in Hong Kong.
Database querying, schema design, and optimization. MySQL and PostgreSQL most common in HK.
Version control and collaborative development. Essential for any software engineering role.
Understanding of agile methodologies, sprint planning, and iterative development. Standard practice in most HK tech teams.
Deploying and managing applications on cloud platforms. Azure is particularly common in HK enterprise environments.
Containerization for development and deployment. Increasingly expected in modern software teams.
Design and implementation of RESTful APIs. Core skill for backend and full-stack roles.
Writing and maintaining test suites. Understanding of TDD principles.
Essential for technical documentation, code comments, and communication in multinational teams.
Preferred for local company communication. Less critical at MNCs and tech startups.

roles.growthOutlook: HK firms increasingly investing in data-driven decision making. BI skills in demand across banking, logistics, and retail. Median entry salary ~HK$510K/year (IBM/Coursera data).

requiredSkills.description: Advanced query writing, performance optimization, and data modeling. The single most essential BI skill.
Build, publish, and maintain interactive dashboards and reports. Power BI is especially valued in HK enterprises.
Data manipulation with pandas, ETL scripting, and automation of reporting workflows.
Design and maintain data pipelines using SSIS, Azure Data Factory, or Apache Airflow. Core to the BI developer role.
Understanding of star/snowflake schema design, dimensional modeling, and data warehouse architecture.
Advanced Excel including Power Query and Power Pivot for data modeling and ad-hoc analysis.
Azure Synapse, Databricks, or AWS Redshift for cloud-based data warehousing and analytics.
Translate business requirements into technical solutions and present insights to stakeholders.
Work within agile teams, participate in sprint planning, and deliver BI features iteratively.
Valuable for understanding business requirements from local stakeholders.

roles.roleTitleZh: 人工智能/機器學習工程師
roles.growthOutlook: Explosive growth in HK, accelerated by GenAI adoption and government smart city initiatives. HKSTP and Cyberport incubating AI startups. Strong demand from fintech, insurtech, and enterprise AI teams.

requiredSkills.description: Primary language for ML/AI development. Deep proficiency in NumPy, pandas, and ML frameworks essential.
Build, train, evaluate, and deploy ML models. Understanding of supervised and unsupervised learning techniques.
Extract and prepare training data from databases. Feature engineering at scale.
Experience with transformer models, LLMs, and NLP techniques. Increasingly expected as GenAI adoption grows.
Deploy models to production using Docker, MLflow, or cloud ML platforms. Understanding of model monitoring.

requiredSkills.skillName: Cloud AI Services (Azure AI / AWS SageMaker)
requiredSkills.description: Leverage cloud-based AI services for model training and deployment. Azure AI is particularly relevant in HK.
Collaborative development, experiment tracking, and code review in ML teams.
Strong foundation in probability theory, statistical inference, and experimental design for model validation.
Statistical computing. Some research-oriented AI roles prefer R for prototyping.
Essential for reading research papers, technical documentation, and collaboration in the global AI community.
Useful for collaborating with mainland Chinese AI teams and accessing Chinese-language AI resources.

roles.growthOutlook: Steady demand in HK across all sectors. Government T-contract projects, financial services tech, and enterprise digital transformation driving hiring. Entry via Project Coordinator/PMO roles for graduates.

requiredSkills.description: Lead sprint planning, daily standups, retrospectives, and manage sprint backlogs. Scrum Master certification valued.
Gantt charts, critical path analysis, milestone planning, and project budgeting. PMP or PRINCE2 knowledge expected.
Track project progress, manage backlogs, and document project artifacts. Standard tools in HK IT teams.
Manage expectations across technical teams, business stakeholders, and external vendors. Create status reports and executive presentations.
Identify, assess, and mitigate project risks. Create risk registers and contingency plans.
Understanding of the full software development lifecycle including requirements gathering, design, development, testing, and deployment.
Understanding of cloud infrastructure concepts to manage cloud migration and SaaS implementation projects.
Manage project budgets, track costs, and plan resource allocation across project phases.
Essential for communication in multinational IT teams, vendor management, and documentation.
Required for local stakeholder communication, government projects, and managing local vendor relationships.
Increasingly important for managing cross-border IT projects and mainland vendor relationships.
